title: Vision Statistics
description: There are several statistics for each object type. You can use the VGet command to retrieve a statistical value from your SPEL program.
notes:
The following vision object Statistics results are available.

Note:

Camera and Robot statistics depend on whether there is a calibration associated with a sequence.

For Blob, Correlation, Edge, and Geometric objects, you can retrieve statistics for each result. But be aware that if the results are not sorted, then the statistics could be invalid.

For example:

For i = 1 to numFound

VGet seq1.blob01.AreaMin(i), min(i)

VGet seq1.blob01.AreaMax(i), max(i)

Next i

Vision Object	Statistics Results Supported from SPEL

examples:
For i = 1 to numFound VGet seq1.blob01.AreaMin(i), min(i) VGet seq1.blob01.AreaMax(i), max(i)

Next i

Real Mean, StdDev

VGet Seq1.Blob01.AreaMean(1), Mean

VGet Seq1.Blob01.AreaStdDev(1), StdDev

Print "Area mean = ", Mean

Print "Area standard deviation = ", StdDev
